rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Protective Order Against Plaintiffs Notice Of Deposition Of Alta Mechanical And Request For Production Of Documents And Against Plaintiffs Request For Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 1. Intervenors¿ motion for protective order against plaintiffs¿ notice of deposition of Alta Mechanical and request for production of documents and against plaintiffs¿ request for discovery responses is granted. The logic of the cases discussing intervention by an insurer when its insured is a suspended corporation is that the intervening insurer is representing only its own interests, not the interests of the suspended corporation and thus the intervening insurer does not take on the discovery obligations that the suspended corporation would have if it had been defending itself. (See, e.g., Reliance Insurance Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal. App. 4th 383). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BETTY PETERSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Alta Mechanical To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Alta Mechanical to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition is denied. The logic of the cases discussing intervention by an insurer when its insured is a suspended corporation is that the intervening insurer is representing only its own interests, not the interests of the suspended corporation and thus the intervening insurer does not take on the discovery obligations that the suspended corporation would have if it had been defending itself. (See, e.g., Reliance Insurance Co. v. Superior Court (2000) 84 Cal. App. 4th 383). = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREA CODY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deft Certainteed Corporation To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel defendant Certainteed Corp. to produce is custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition and for sanctions is denied. Discovery is closed and plaintiff failed to move to take discovery within 30 days of trial. (See C.C.P. §§ 2024.020(a) and 2024.050; Pelton-Shepherd Industries v. Delta Packaging Products (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 1568). Moreover, plaintiff did not demonstrate that it met and conferred in good faith before filing this motion. All requests for sanctions are denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN R. LEONARD et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 4. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp. to provide further responses to plaintiffs¿ requests for admission and form interrogatories is continued on the court¿s own motion to September 14 at 9am to give the parties additional time to meet and confer. If all issues are not resolved before the, by 4:30pm on September 13 the parties must send an email stating, without argument, the RFA¿s and corresponding form interrogatories that remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing at 9am on September 14. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS METCALF et al VS. PARKER-HANNIFIN CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Allow Destructive Testing Of Pathology Materials
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 7, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00a.m., Line 5. Defendants Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. and Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion to allow destructive testing of pathology materials is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion upon a proper showing. The moving papers do not explain precisely what testing is sought to be done, what pathology materials are sought to be tested, or whether any other defendants have been contacted so as to avoid a situation where other defendants desire their own destructive testing and there is insufficient pathology materials for them to test. Counsel are encouraged to meet and confer to discuss all of these issues within the next week. = (220/HEK)
